title: Secure ≥1 publishing channel + ≥1 validating educator; validate AnkiWeb policy
context: The open-flashcards project cannot publish decks until at least one distribution channel is confirmed and at least one validating educator is identified. Without a confirmed channel, all deck authoring work may be wasted. AnkiWeb is a primary candidate but its policy on automated or attributed community uploads must be verified. A time-boxed search with explicit kill/pivot criteria prevents indefinite blocking.
objective: Research and confirm ≥1 publishing channel willing to accept clean-provenance, openly-licensed, attributed decks; validate AnkiWeb policy; identify ≥1 educator/learner who will serve as the first subject's accuracy validator; document kill/pivot criteria.
acceptanceCriteria: Evaluates candidate channels in priority order (GitHub release → AnkiWeb → OER/CrowdAnki partner) with each channel's acceptance posture, applying the PLAN.md decision tree.
Confirms ≥1 channel will accept clean-provenance, openly-licensed, attributed decks, and validates AnkiWeb shared-deck policy / account terms if AnkiWeb is chosen.
Identifies ≥1 validating educator/learner who confirms the first subject is genuinely needed and will check accuracy.
Records the channel's required deck metadata, license display requirements, and any rate/automation limits.
States the time-box and kill/pivot criteria; on success, flips verifiedNeed=true for tasks targeting that channel/subject.
Published as a versioned document, CC-BY-4.0.
output: A research report (docs/channel-partner-research.md) documenting evaluated channels, confirmed channel(s), educator contacts, policy validation outcomes, and kill/pivot criteria.
